title: Market Brief — Thursday, June 18, 2026
summary: GPU availability is loosening fast across every class. Util-avail stands at 39% for A100, 32% for H100, 39% for H200, and 44% for B200, meaning no tier is anywhere near scarcity. B200 posted the biggest daily price move at +4.4% to $4.38/hr, a counter-trend bounce amid a 34.8pp utilization drop over 30 days that signals demand is cratering even as spot prices tick up on short-term volatility.
paragraphs: GPU availability is loosening fast across every class. Util-avail stands at 39% for A100, 32% for H100, 39% for H200, and 44% for B200, meaning no tier is anywhere near scarcity. B200 posted the biggest daily price move at +4.4% to $4.38/hr, a counter-trend bounce amid a 34.8pp utilization drop over 30 days that signals demand is cratering even as spot prices tick up on short-term volatility.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), running Llama 70B on H100 costs roughly $8.78/M tokens, making DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.34/M output a loss of approximately $8.44 per million tokens. DeepSeek: Burning Cash. Meta Llama 3.3 70B at $0.32/M output: Burning Cash. OpenAI gpt-oss-120b at $0.18/M: Burning Cash. Anthropic Haiku 4.5 at $5/M output on presumably efficient infra: Neutral. OpenAI o3 Pro at $80/M output: Strong. Google Gemini 2.5 Flash at $2.50/M: Neutral.

B200 spot at $4.38/hr is overvalued given 44% util-avail, 73% of offerings sitting idle, and 60.7% annualized vol, meaning prices have room to fall further. A100 at $1.01/hr is undervalued for cost-conscious inference, with the lowest raw token cost at $5.12/M and the only GPU class delivering positive margins at the High pricing tier, so the trade is long A100 capacity, short B200 exposure.
bullets: 44% util-avail on B200 marks the loosest supply of any GPU class today.
32% util-avail on H100 is well above scarcity, down 22.1pp in 30 days.
$3.20/M tokens makes B200 the cheapest raw inference cost for Llama 70B.
170.5% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI makes it the riskiest compute position.
21.78 on the LLMflation Index means frontier token prices have fallen 78% since GPT-4 launch.
$0.18/M output for OpenAI gpt-oss-120b undercuts TCO-adjusted A100 cost by roughly 96%.
